changes: red rice stays closest in chewy whole-grain feel
millets feel lighter and less chewy
quinoa cooks faster but tastes different
caution: Cooking time and water ratio can change even when the meal still works.

changes: red rice flakes stay closest to the flattened-rice idea
millet or oats breakfasts are heavier than poha
the dish may lose the soft flake texture
caution: If the recipe depends on the quick soft poha texture, suggest a different breakfast instead of forcing a weak swap.

changes: split moong dal is the closest in flavor and cooking behavior
toor dal works but changes the texture slightly
chana dal stays firmer and changes the final feel
caution: Moong dal is milder and softer than many other dals, so swaps are not fully identical.

changes: in dosa and idli batters, swapping urad dal changes fermentation, softness, and lift
other dals are not equivalent in batter-heavy recipes
caution: Be much more cautious with urad dal than with most other legumes. Batter-heavy recipes are the main no-swap zone.

best_swaps: choose a recipe that does not rely heavily on onion
changes: onion adds sweetness, moisture, and background flavor
omission can work better than pretending there is a perfect equal substitute
caution: In simple dishes where onion sweetness matters, the better answer may be a different recipe.

best_swaps: use less coconut and rely on the rest of the base
changes: light coconut milk can preserve some coconut character while thinning richness
using less coconut reduces richness, sweetness, and mouthfeel
caution: If coconut is part of the identity of the dish, the result can feel like a different meal.

pcos: use less coconut and rely on the rest of the base
high-triglycerides: use less coconut and rely on the rest of the base

changes: buttermilk keeps tang but makes the result thinner
thicker recipes may lose body if curd is replaced directly
caution: Check whether the recipe needs thickness or just tang before swapping.

best_swaps: omit if only a very small finishing amount is used
changes: the biggest loss is aroma and South Indian flavor character
the dish may still cook fine but taste less distinct
caution: For triglyceride-focused answers, prioritize moderation and cooking method rather than pretending oil choice alone solves the problem.

high-triglycerides: omit if only a very small finishing amount is used

best_swaps: reduce sourness and rely on other spices
changes: raw mango is one of the closest in-vault souring options
tamarind has a darker deeper flavor than raw mango
caution: Tamarind-forward dishes may still work with a substitute, but the result can become a different style of meal.
